text.title: Secrets Management Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Secrets Management methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Long-lived static credentials (access keys, service-account JSON keys, plaintext API tokens) MUST NOT be stored in code, env files committed to repos, or CI variables. All access uses cloud identity (IRSA / Workload Identity / OIDC).

rationale.source: AWS Security Best Practices; Verizon DBIR 2024

rule.rationale: Static keys are the #1 cloud breach vector. They leak via repo pushes, screen recordings, log dumps. Identity-based auth has no key to leak.

rule.statement: Kubernetes secrets injection MUST use External Secrets Operator (ESO) or the platform's Secret Store CSI driver. Plaintext kubectl create secret is forbidden in production.
rule.rationale: ESO syncs from any backend (Vault, ASM, GSM) to a native Secret object, decoupling apps from backend choice and enabling automatic rotation.

rule.statement: All non-trivial credentials rotate on a 30-day or shorter cycle. Rotation MUST be automated; manual rotation is treated as a control gap.

rationale.source: NIST SP 800-53 IA-5; CIS Controls v8

rule.rationale: Long credential lifetime increases breach blast radius. Automated rotation is the only practical control at scale.

rule.statement: Every secret-accessing principal has a least-privilege policy: explicit secret paths, no wildcards, scoped to the calling service identity.

rationale.source: AWS IAM least-privilege; Vault policy patterns

rule.rationale: A broad policy ('secrets/*') turns a single compromise into total exfiltration; tight scoping limits blast radius.

rule.statement: Audit logging MUST be enabled on the secrets backend with logs shipped to a separate, immutable store. Logs include WHO accessed WHICH secret WHEN, with no PII redaction of the principal.
rule.rationale: Audit logs are the forensic record after a breach. Logs in the same account as the backend can be tampered with by the same attacker.

rule.statement: Container images MUST NOT bake secrets into layers. Secrets are mounted at runtime via volume or env from a secret-store reference, never via ENV directives in Dockerfile.
rule.rationale: Image layers are world-readable to anyone with pull access; baked secrets persist in registries forever.
